summary: Surah Al-An'am affirms Allah's oneness, creative power, and knowledge of all things. It condemns idolatry and superstitions, and recounts lessons from previous nations and prophets, especially Ibrahim. The surah emphasizes following divine guidance, avoiding innovation in faith, and upholding justice, mercy, and truth. It details lawful and unlawful foods, warns against following conjecture, and calls for sincerity in worship and obedience, reminding that every soul will be accountable to Allah alone.
